Toledo Express Airport Wikipedia; Northwest Airlink Metroliner nonstop to DTW
Toledo Express Airport Wikipedia; USAir Express BAe Jetstream 31 to DAY and IND
Toledo Express Airport Wikipedia; USAir Boeing 727/DC-9 nonstop to PIT discontinued after deregulation downsizing

note: Peak 1997 with 679,841 enplanements; American Airlines (Envoy Air) last legacy carrier, final flight to ORD on September 6 2022; airport now served only by Allegiant Air leisure routes

lostDestinations.source: Youngstown-Warren Regional Airport Wikipedia; Northwest discontinued DTW service via Akron-Canton September 2002, ending all scheduled service
Youngstown-Warren Regional Airport Wikipedia; United/USAir served YNG in late 1990s alongside Northwest; all gone by 2002
Youngstown-Warren Regional Airport Wikipedia; three daily network carriers—United, Northwest, USAir—all departed by 2002
Youngstown-Warren Regional Airport Wikipedia; Allegiant launched MCO/SFB 2006, added PIE/SRQ/MYR, ceased all YNG service January 4 2018
Youngstown-Warren Regional Airport Wikipedia; Allegiant flew PIE from YNG until January 2018
Youngstown-Warren Regional Airport Wikipedia; Allegiant seasonal MYR ended when they exited YNG January 2018

note: YNG has had no scheduled commercial service since January 4 2018; in 2016 Aerodynamics Inc. briefly served ORD for six weeks; airport seeking $1.4M DOT grant to restore service

lostDestinations.source: Mansfield Lahm Regional Airport Wikipedia; Allegheny served Mansfield until 1969 then Allegheny Commuter until mid-1970s
Mansfield Lahm Regional Airport Wikipedia; regional commuter routes to Columbus included in 1970s Allegheny network
Mansfield Lahm Regional Airport Wikipedia; Allegheny served Mansfield on multi-stop Ohio routes through 1969

note: MFD had TWA DC-3 service from 1947; Lake Central took over 1953; Allegheny took over 1968 then Allegheny Commuter until early 1970s; airport now general aviation only with no commercial service

lostDestinations.source: Terre Haute Regional Airport Wikipedia; Britt Airways flew ORD-HUF-IND routing 1984-1989 then as Continental Express
Terre Haute Regional Airport Wikipedia; Britt added STL service in 1981; service ended when Britt absorbed into Continental late 1980s
Terre Haute Regional Airport Wikipedia; Chicago and Southern flew HUF-Chicago and HUF-Evansville, merged into Delta 1953 service ended 1954

note: Last commercial service: Great Lakes Aviation as United Express to ORD, ended July 10 1999; airport now general aviation only; TWA flew HUF to IND and STL 1944-1967

lostDestinations.source: Delaware County Regional Airport Wikipedia; Allegheny Commuter flew MIE-IND-ORD routing in early 1980s
Delaware County Regional Airport Wikipedia; Britt Airways served MIE on multi-stop Indiana routes mid-1980s

note: MIE had Allegheny Commuter service to ORD and IND in early 1980s; Britt Airways continued through ~1985; Great Lakes Aviation operated ORD service in 1990s; airport lost all commercial service by late 1990s

lostDestinations.source: Dayton International Airport Wikipedia; Piedmont hub operated nonstops to 27 cities including BOS until USAir dehubbed DAY Jan 1992
Dayton International Airport Wikipedia; Piedmont route maps Dec 1988 showed DAY-LAX nonstop, ended with 1991 dehubbing
Dayton International Airport Wikipedia; Piedmont nonstop to MIA on DAY hub network pre-dehubbing
Dayton International Airport Wikipedia; Southwest last Dayton flight June 3 2017 including MDW service
Dayton International Airport Wikipedia; Southwest ended BWI/DEN/MCO/TPA nonstops April 1 2015
Dayton International Airport Wikipedia; Air Canada Express ended DAY-YYZ May 1 2011

detail: Piedmont Airlines opened a Midwest hub at DAY July 1 1982 with nonstops to 27+ cities; USAir dehubbed in August 1991 cutting daily departures from 73 to 20; DAY has steadily lost routes since and now serves only 12 destinations with 4 airlines.
source: Dayton International Airport Wikipedia; UPI Archives Aug 19 1991 'USAir to drop Dayton hub'; avgeekery.com Dayton hub history

lostDestinations.source: Akron-Canton Airport Wikipedia; Delta suspended ATL service in 2020 citing declining demand; ScienceDirect 2025 CAK analysis
Akron-Canton Airport Wikipedia; Southwest exited CAK in 2017 consolidating at CLE; CAK passenger traffic hit lowest since 2004
Akron-Canton Airport Wikipedia; Southwest flew CAK to DCA before 2017 exit
Akron-Canton Airport Wikipedia; CAK had Southwest nonstops to ATL/MDW/LGA/EWR/HOU/DCA/CLT/MCO/TPA before 2017 exit
Akron-Canton Airport Wikipedia; Allegiant entered CAK May 2015 then relocated to CLE in 2017
Akron-Canton Airport Wikipedia; Spirit ended MCO/MYR/RSW service at CAK in 2022

detail: CAK lost Southwest Airlines (25 flights/day to 9 destinations) and Allegiant Air in 2017, reducing traffic to its lowest since 2004; Delta ended its long-running Atlanta service in 2020; as of May 2018 CAK had the 2nd-fastest declining passenger count of any US airport; now served by American and United only.
source: Akron-Canton Airport Wikipedia; akroncantonairport.com newsroom; ScienceDirect Jan 2025 CAK analysis article

lostDestinations.source: Lima Allen County Airport Wikipedia; Allegheny Commuter continued post-Lake Central until early 1970s on Columbus-Dayton-Lima routes
Lima Allen County Airport Wikipedia; DC-3s provided service to Columbus, Cleveland, Detroit, Dayton in 1960s
Lima Allen County Airport Wikipedia; multi-stop Ohio network DC-3s to Cleveland
Lima Allen County Airport Wikipedia; Lake Central DC-3 service to Detroit until 1968 Allegheny merger

detail: Lima had Lake Central Airlines DC-3 service starting 1953-54 with connections to Columbus, Cleveland, Detroit, and Dayton; Allegheny continued after the 1968 Lake Central merger; Allegheny Commuter operated the final service until the early 1970s; the airport has had no commercial scheduled service since approximately 1972.
source: Lima Allen County Airport Wikipedia; businessviewmagazine.com Allen County Airport profile

lostDestinations.source: Zanesville Municipal Airport Wikipedia; TWA began service Nov 1 1947 with two daily PIT departures; Lake Central took over 1953
Zanesville Municipal Airport Wikipedia; TWA flew ZZV-CMH with DC-3s until Lake Central took over April 1953
Zanesville Municipal Airport Wikipedia; Lake Central 1953-1967 then Allegheny; last airline flight early January 1970

detail: TWA began service November 1 1947 with four daily departures (two to Pittsburgh, two to Columbus); Lake Central took over April 1953 using DC-3s and Martin 2-0-2/4-0-4 aircraft through 1967; Allegheny Airlines (which acquired Lake Central) operated the final service until early January 1970.

lostDestinations.source: Monroe County Airport Wikipedia; received federal EAS funding until 1997 when DOT cut subsidies due to proximity to Indianapolis International Airport
Monroe County Airport Wikipedia; commercial service historically included Chicago routes prior to EAS wind-down

detail: Monroe County Airport received Essential Air Service federal funding until 1997 when the DOT cut subsidies because the airport is within 70 miles of Indianapolis International Airport; after losing EAS designation it was no longer economically viable for any carrier to serve, and all scheduled service ended.

lostDestinations.source: Purdue University Airport Wikipedia; last regular scheduled service December 2002–February 15 2004; EAS removed June 1999
Purdue University Airport Wikipedia; Indiana Airways flew LAF-IND-FWA-CVG Nov 1977-March 1980
Purdue University Airport Wikipedia; Air Wisconsin flew LAF-ORD and LAF-FWA Feb 1971-Summer 1985

detail: Purdue University Airport lost its EAS eligibility in June 1999 due to proximity to Indianapolis; final regular scheduled service ended February 15 2004; in May 2024 Southern Airways Express relaunched ORD service (replaced by SkyWest/United Express August 2025), making LAF again 'diminished' rather than fully lost.
source: Purdue University Airport Wikipedia; purdue.edu airport news May 2024

lostDestinations.source: Gary/Chicago International Airport Wikipedia; Allegiant launched Feb 15 2012, ended service August 10 2013 citing low demand
Gary/Chicago International Airport Wikipedia; SkyValue hub Dec 2006-April 2007 flew to PIE/GSO/BDL
Gary/Chicago International Airport Wikipedia; SkyValue seasonal GYY hub to MYR among destinations
Gary/Chicago International Airport Wikipedia; SkyValue flew GYY to Hartford CT and Greensboro NC

detail: Gary/Chicago International Airport has had no scheduled commercial passenger service since Allegiant Air's final flight on August 10 2013; prior carriers included Pan Am, Hooters Air, SkyValue Airlines, and Skybus Airlines; November 2025 terminal upgrades began to attract new service in 2026.

lostDestinations.source: Evansville Regional Airport Wikipedia; Delta/Northwest merged; Memphis hub service ended 2009 as cost-cutting post-merger
Evansville Regional Airport Wikipedia; United pulled EVV-ORD service November 8 2021 among 15 airports
Evansville Regional Airport Wikipedia; Delta suspended Detroit service from EVV during COVID period
Evansville Regional Airport Wikipedia; Comair/Delta Connection served CVG hub before service consolidation
Evansville Regional Airport Wikipedia; American paused EVV-ORD/MDW flights March 3 2022; direct Chicago service absent until American resumed ORD September 4 2024

detail: Evansville lost nonstop Chicago service twice: United ended ORD service November 2021 and American paused Chicago flights March 2022, leaving the airport with no direct Chicago connection for 2.5 years until American resumed ORD service September 4 2024; Detroit and Memphis hub connections are permanently gone.
source: Evansville Regional Airport Wikipedia; flyevv.com announcements Nov 2021 and Sep 2024

lastCarrier: American Airlines / United Airlines / Allegiant / Breeze

lostDestinations.source: Fort Wayne International Airport Wikipedia; US Airways ended PIT service as Pittsburgh hub de-peaked; ATA briefly substituted IND before closing regional division early 2005
journalgazette.net Oct 2023; American discontinued FWA-PHL August 2016 citing low passenger-to-seat ratio
Fort Wayne International Airport Wikipedia; Allegiant ended LAS and PHX service from FWA 2008 due to fuel price volatility
Fort Wayne International Airport Wikipedia; Allegiant ended PHX service FWA 2008
Dayton International Airport Wikipedia; Dec 1988 Piedmont hub route maps show FWA-DAY nonstop; ended 1991 dehubbing

detail: Fort Wayne lost Pittsburgh hub connection circa 2002 when US Airways dehubbed PIT; Philadelphia direct service ended August 2016; the airport recovered by adding American service to CLT and DFW and United service to ORD and EWR, but remains without several legacy hub connections it once held.
source: Fort Wayne International Airport Wikipedia; journalgazette.net Oct 2023

lostDestinations.source: Kokomo Municipal Airport Wikipedia; Lake Central DC-3s flew ORD-Gary-Lafayette-Kokomo routing from ~1950
Kokomo Municipal Airport Wikipedia; Allegheny inherited Lake Central routes after 1968 merger, pulled out 1969-70

detail: Kokomo had Delta DC-3 service starting late 1947 and Turner/Lake Central service from ~1950 on multi-stop Indiana routes to Chicago and Indianapolis; Allegheny Airlines ended service circa 1969-1970 after acquiring Lake Central; the EAS program listed Kokomo-Logansport-Peru as eligible until 1989 but no carrier provided service after Allegheny's exit.
